title: geniostoma tinifolium var. lanaiense
intro: Geniostoma tinifolium var. lanaiense, also known as the Lanai vine, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been utilized to treat wounds and skin infections. The health benefits of Geniostoma tinifolium var. lanaiense include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a salve or infused in oil to utilize its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
